title: Spicy Velvety Mexican Jalapeno Stir-fry
description: A brunch-mood Polish dish featuring jalapenos in a velvety sauce that is perfect for pescatarian diets and can be baked to perfection.

ingredients.name: Jalapeno peppers
ingredients.notes: Choose from mild to very hot based on preference.

ingredients.name: Garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: Can be minced or whole, depending on personal preference and cooking time

ingredients.name: Salsa verde (optional)
ingredients.notes: For added flavor without adding too much heat

ingredients.name: Tomato paste
ingredients.notes: Can also use tomato sauce if preferred

ingredients.name: Chicken broth or water
ingredients.notes: To help create the velvety texture

ingredients.name: Heavy cream
ingredients.notes: Optional, for a richer, velvety flavor and texture

ingredients.name: Coconut milk or heavy whipping cream (for non-dairy option)
ingredients.notes: For those following a vegan or dairy-free diet

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Neutral flavor, for sautéing and baking purposes

instructions: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Rinse the jalapeno peppers under cold water and remove seeds for milder heat or leave them in for spicier salsa.

Cut the jalapenos into thin strips and finely chop the garlic cloves. If using a mix of tomato paste and chicken broth, simmer these ingredients together until reduced to about 1 cup. Add any optional Salsa Verde for extra flavor.
In a large baking dish, combine the cut peppers with chopped garlic, remaining tomato paste or sauce, and cooked tomatoes broth mixture. Mix well to ensure all the pieces are evenly coated.
Sprinkle salt and pepper over the entire surface of the ingredients, including on top of jalapenos for even distribution of flavor and spice throughout.

In a separate pot, heat olive oil until hot. Add the coconut cream or heavy cream to it while heating up; this will help thicken as we bake. Once combined, pour this mixture over the dish with peppers, ensuring that all ingredients are fully coated in sauce.
Place the baking dish into the preheated oven and let cook for about 20-25 minutes until the peppers are tender and slightly crisp from baking on top.
After 20-25 minutes, turn off the heat. Let it rest for another 10-15 minutes before serving. Optionally, garnish with fresh herbs like cilantro or parsley to add a pop of color and freshness.
